title: PGlite + Valkey + DuckDB Data Architecture
context: Tribunus requires three distinct runtime capabilities that no single database provides: Durable intelligence (Codex with diagnostic packets, dharma ledger, packet provenance, vector embeddings), Live coordination (multiple agents and humans need real-time shared state), and Retrospective analysis (product questions about packet propagation, framework failures, agent route quality). SQLite was the initial local store. Replaced with PGlite for vector search (pgvector), JSON document support (JSONB), and extension capabilities. Valkey was added for distributed coordination. DuckDB was added for columnar analytics.
